data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify's infrastructure is based on a microservices architecture, enabling independent deployment and scaling of different functionalities such as recommendation engines and music streaming.
data.technical_spec: RESTful APIs, gRPC communication protocols, and containerized services using Docker.

data.title: Distributed Databases
data.description: Utilization of distributed database systems to store user data, playlists, and music tracks for high availability and fault tolerance.
data.technical_spec: Apache Cassandra, PostgreSQL, and in-memory databases like Redis.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: A CDN is employed to efficiently deliver music content and minimize latency for users worldwide.
data.technical_spec: Akamai CDN for global content distribution.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Sophisticated load balancing strategies ensuring even distribution of server requests, reducing downtime and improving service reliability.
data.technical_spec: Nginx and HAProxy for load balancing.

data.title: Real-Time Data Processing
data.description: Architecture supports real-time data processing for features like user activity feeds and personalized music recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Apache Kafka, Spark Streaming for handling real-time data flows.
